description: Commercially and economically significant palms cultivated globally for food, oil, fiber, and raw materials. Includes coconut, date palm, oil palm, sago palm, rattan, betel nut, palmyra, and royal palms. Many species are critically important in Indian agriculture and cuisine.
meta_description: Complete taxonomy of economically important palms: coconut, date, oil palm, sago, betel, palmyra, rattan and more.

genera.description: Monotypic genus containing the coconut palm, one of the most economically important plants in the world, especially in tropical coastal Asia. Called Nariyal/Naariyal in Hindi, Thengai in Tamil, Tengu in Telugu.
Two species of oil palms producing the world's most consumed vegetable oil. Elaeis guineensis dominates global palm oil production. Called Tel Tal in Bengali.

Genus of 14 species of palms native from the Canary Islands through North Africa, the Middle East, and South Asia to Southeast Asia. Phoenix dactylifera is the true date palm, while P. sylvestris is the Indian date palm / Khajur used for jaggery and toddy.

species.common_name: Indian Date Palm / Silver Date Palm / Khajoor
Senegal Date Palm / African Wild Date

genera.description: Large fan palms of enormous economic importance in South and Southeast Asia. Borassus flabellifer (Palmyra/Taal/Taad) is the state tree of Tamil Nadu and provides sugar, fruit, timber, and fiber.

species.common_name: African Palmyra Palm / African Fan Palm

genera.description: Monotypic genus: the only mangrove palm. Found in coastal estuaries and tidal rivers from South Asia to the Pacific. Nypa fruticans is important for thatching, sugar/toddy production, and coastal protection.

Palms of Southeast Asia and the Pacific that accumulate massive quantities of starch in their trunk before flowering. Metroxylon sagu is the primary source of sago starch, a staple food in lowland New Guinea.

The largest palm genus with 500+ species, predominantly climbing palms used for rattan furniture, basketry, and construction material. Extensively traded in India and Southeast Asia. Called Bet/Rattan in Hindi.
Palms of South and Southeast Asia, best known for Areca catechu — the betel nut or supari. The fourth most consumed psychoactive substance worldwide. Called Supari in Hindi, Paakku in Tamil.

Distinctive palms with bipinnate (twice-divided) leaves resembling a fishtail. Native to South and Southeast Asia. Called Jhula Tal in Hindi. Flowers once and dies (monocarpic). Used for sago and jaggery.

species.common_name: Toddy Palm / Wine Palm / Jhula Tal

genera.description: Tall, stately palms native to the Caribbean and Central America, widely planted in tropical avenues worldwide. The national tree of Cuba. Commonly planted on Indian roads and estates.
Fan palms native from East Africa to Southeast Asia and Australia, used for thatch, weaving, and timber. L. chinensis is widely planted in Indian parks and gardens.
Climbing rattan palms, formerly in Calamus, producing dragon's blood resin used in varnishes and traditional medicine. Important in Asian rattan trade.
Fan palms native to the Americas. Sabal palmetto is the state tree of South Carolina and Florida. Several species are important for cabbage palm hearts (swamp cabbage), and thatch.
Cold-hardy palms native to the Himalayas, China, and Japan. Trachycarpus fortunei is widely cultivated globally for its extreme cold tolerance. T. martianus is native to the Himalayas.
